title: Synthesizing large memory blocks takes a really long time.
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

e.g. synthesize:

```
// represents memory controller (plus memory that the memory is controlling)
module mem(
    input we, clk,
    input [15:0] read_addr,
    input [15:0] write_addr,
    output [15:0] read_data,
    input [15:0] write_data,

    input [15:0] oob_write_addr,
    input [15:0] oob_write_data,
    input oob_wen
);
    reg [15:0] mem [65536];

    always @(posedge clk) begin
        if (we) begin
            mem[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
        if(oob_wen) begin
            mem[oob_write_addr] <= oob_write_data;
        end
    end
    assign read_data = mem[read_addr];
endmodule
```

And use following yosys script:
```
read_verilog -sv src/mem.sv
synth
```

## Expected behavior

Given that it's a single memory block, I would have expected that one could synthesize for one memory location, and just replicate that 65,526 times for the other memory locations?

## Actual behavior

Treats each memory location as its own special snowflake, running full optimization passes on every single memroy location, which takes a really long time.
